extraction_scope: All source files across Java Swing MVP, Node.js WebSocket relay, and Vue.js frontend components

business_domains.description: Capture, storage, display, and transmission of individual person identity data including name, date of birth, and customer identifiers.

rules.rule: A person record must include: first name (Vorname), last name (Name/Nachname), and date of birth (Geburtsdatum).
All person data fields are initialised to null, representing an 'empty/not-yet-entered' state before user interaction.
Person data fields synchronise with the domain model immediately on every character insertion or deletion (real-time binding).
rules.source_element: bind(JTextComponent, ModelProperties) — insertUpdate and removeUpdate listeners

rules.rule: On successful form submission, all person data fields (first name, last name, date of birth) are cleared to empty string.
rules.source_element: EventEmitter subscription lambda in PocPresenter constructor

rules.rule: The mock person dataset contains exactly 5 persons: Hans Mayer (Trogen, DOB 1981-01-08), Linda Reitmayr (Hof, DOB 1979-05-12), Karl May (Berlin, DOB 1964-11-02), Jens Mueller (Potsdam, DOB 1999-04-21), Steffi Ruckmueller (Templin, DOB 1961-11-05).
Each person in the dataset has a unique Kundennummer (customer number): 79423984, 67485246, 13725246, 41125291, 31228193.

business_domains.description: Capture and storage of a person's gender as a mutually exclusive selection among three options: Female (Weiblich), Male (Männlich), and Diverse (Divers).

rules.rule: Gender has exactly three valid options: Weiblich (Female), Männlich (Male), Divers (Diverse).
rules.source_element: JRadioButton female, male, diverse field declarations

rules.rule: Only one gender option may be selected at a time (mutual exclusivity).
rules.source_element: ButtonGroup gender containing female, male, diverse

rules.rule: The default gender selection on form initialisation and after form reset is Female (Weiblich).
After a successful form submission, gender state is explicitly reset: Female=selected, Male=deselected, Diverse=deselected.
Gender is stored in the domain model as three independent Boolean-typed properties (MALE, FEMALE, DIVERSE), not as a single enumerated value.
When transmitted via HTTP, gender Boolean flags are serialised as their string representations (e.g., 'true'/'false') in the JSON payload.
rules.source_element: action() — model.get(val).getField().toString() applied uniformly to all properties

rules.rule: In the API contract, FEMALE, MALE, and DIVERSE are all declared as string fields (not boolean), confirming the string serialisation approach.
rules.source_element: components/schemas/PostObject properties: FEMALE, MALE, DIVERSE (type: string)

business_domains.description: Capture, storage, and display of residential or contact address details including street, house number, postal code, and city.

rules.rule: Address data consists of three components: Street (Strasse), Postal Code (PLZ/ZIP), and City (Ort).
In the Vue.js search form, address data is extended with a house number (Hausnummer) field as a separate input.
rules.source_element: formdata.hausnr input field and search_space hausnr property

rules.rule: In search results display, street and house number are concatenated into a single column ('Strasse': item.street + ' ' + item.hausnr).
Postal code (ZIP) is matched by exact string equality during person search; all other address fields use case-insensitive partial (substring) matching.
rules.source_element: searchPerson() — element.zip == formdata.zip (strict equality) vs. indexOf() for street/ort/hausnr

rules.rule: After successful submission in the Swing form, all address fields (street, ZIP, city) are cleared to empty string.
A Postfach (PO Box) field is available in the Vue form as an optional alternative address component.

business_domains.description: Capture, storage, selection, and transmission of banking payment details including IBAN, BIC, and payment validity dates. Supports multiple payment recipients (Zahlungsempfänger) per person.

rules.rule: Payment data consists of three fields: IBAN (International Bank Account Number), BIC (Bank Identifier Code), and VALID_FROM (validity start date).
A person can have multiple Zahlungsempfänger (payment recipient) entries, each with its own IBAN, BIC, valid_from date, valid_until date, and type.
Only one Zahlungsempfänger can be active/selected at a time for the current person selection.
IBAN and BIC fields in the Vue.js form are read-only (disabled); they cannot be manually entered and are populated only by selecting a Zahlungsempfänger.
rules.source_element: input v-model='formdata.iban' disabled; input v-model='formdata.bic' disabled

rules.rule: When transmitting person data to Allegro, the full zahlungsempfaenger array is replaced with only the single selected Zahlungsempfänger object.
Payment data (IBAN, BIC) in the Swing form can be freely entered and is cleared to empty string after successful submission.
VALID_FROM (Gültig ab) field is also cleared after successful submission.
The Zahlungsempfänger display table columns are: IBAN, BIC, Gültig ab (valid from).
All IBAN values in the mock dataset follow the German IBAN format (DE + 2 check digits + 18 digits), e.g., DE27100777770209299700.

business_domains.description: Multi-criteria person lookup functionality enabling operators to find person records by any combination of personal, address, or identifier fields.

rules.rule: Person search supports up to six simultaneous criteria: last name, first name, ZIP code, city, street, and house number.
A person is included in results if ANY one of the provided non-empty search criteria produces a match (inclusive OR logic).
rules.source_element: searchPerson() — multiple || conditions in the if statement

rules.rule: String matching for last name, first name, city, street, and house number uses case-insensitive substring (indexOf) matching.
ZIP code matching uses exact equality (==), not substring matching.
An empty search criterion does not participate in matching — only criteria with non-empty (truthy) input values are evaluated.
Search results are reset (cleared to empty array) at the start of each search operation.
The search operates against a local in-memory mock dataset (no backend API call is made for person lookup).

business_domains.description: Workflow for collecting form data, submitting to a backend service, handling the response, and resetting the form state.

rules.rule: Form submission is triggered exclusively by the 'Anordnen' button click event.
All 13 model properties are included in every submission regardless of whether they have been filled in.
The HTTP endpoint for submission is POST http://localhost:8080/post.
The HTTP request body is a flat JSON object with one key-value pair per model property (no nesting).
rules.source_element: post() — generator.writeStartObject() + generator.write(key, value) per entry

rules.rule: If the HTTP response body is non-empty, it is treated as success and displayed in the text area.
If the HTTP response body is empty, the literal error message 'Failed operation' is emitted.
On successful response, the form is fully reset: all text fields cleared, gender reverted to Female.
rules.source_element: EventEmitter subscription lambda in PocPresenter constructor

rules.rule: IOException and InterruptedException thrown during HTTP submission are wrapped in RuntimeException (unhandled gracefully).
rules.source_element: button ActionListener catch blocks — throw new RuntimeException(e)

business_domains.description: Workflow for transferring selected person and payment data from the modern Vue.js search UI to the legacy Allegro Swing application via WebSocket relay.

rules.rule: Data transfer to Allegro is initiated by the 'Nach ALLEGRO übernehmen' button click.
The data packet sent to Allegro always replaces the full Zahlungsempfänger array with only the single selected entry.
The JSON message envelope transmitted via WebSocket has the structure: { target: string, content: object|string }.
The relay server broadcasts every received message to ALL connected WebSocket clients without discrimination.
rules.source_element: connection.on('message') — for loop over clients array, clients[i].sendUTF(json)

rules.rule: The WebSocket connection from the Vue client is established automatically on component mount (not on demand).
Free-text textarea content changes are automatically broadcast to Allegro via WebSocket as 'textarea' target messages.
The WebSocket relay server accepts connections from any origin (null origin check) — suitable for development PoC, not production.

business_domains.description: Formal OpenAPI specification defining the data schema, endpoint, and request/response format for the Allegro backend integration.

rules.rule: The PostObject schema defines exactly 13 fields, all typed as string: FIRST_NAME, LAST_NAME, DATE_OF_BIRTH, STREET, BIC, ORT, ZIP, FEMALE, MALE, DIVERSE, IBAN, VALID_FROM, TEXT_AREA.
The HTTP request body must wrap the PostObject under a 'user' key: { user: PostObject }.
rules.source_element: requestBodies/PostRequest schema — user property referencing PostObject

rules.rule: The HTTP response body wraps the returned data under a 'postObject' key in the response schema.
The API operates on a single endpoint: POST /post at server http://localhost:8080.

workflows.workflow_name: Swing Form Data Entry and Submission
workflows.description: User fills in person, address, gender, and payment fields in the Allegro Swing desktop form and submits to the backend.
steps: 1. Application launches: PocView form is displayed with all fields empty, gender defaulting to Female.
2. User enters text into any field — DocumentListener fires immediately and updates the corresponding model property.
3. User selects a gender radio button — ChangeListener fires and updates the MALE/FEMALE/DIVERSE Boolean model property.
4. User clicks 'Anordnen' button.
5. PocPresenter delegates to PocModel.action().
6. PocModel collects all 13 model properties into a String HashMap.
7. HttpBinService serialises the map as a flat JSON object and POSTs to http://localhost:8080/post.
8. HTTP response body is received.
9. If response is non-empty: EventEmitter emits the response body.
10. If response is empty: EventEmitter emits 'Failed operation'.
11. PocPresenter's event listener receives the event.
12. Text area is populated with the event data.
13. All text fields are cleared; gender reverts to Female selected.

workflows.workflow_name: Vue.js Person Search and Allegro Transfer
workflows.description: Operator uses the Vue.js web UI to search for a person, select their payment details, and transfer the complete record to the Allegro legacy system.
steps: 1. Vue.js app loads; Search component mounts and automatically connects to WebSocket ws://localhost:1337/.
2. Operator enters one or more search criteria (name, ZIP, city, street, etc.) in the person form.
3. Operator clicks 'Suchen' (Search) button.
4. searchPerson() filters local mock dataset using OR logic with case-insensitive partial matching (exact for ZIP).
5. Matching person records are displayed in the results table.
6. Operator clicks a person row to select it — their Zahlungsempfänger list appears in the payment table.
7. Operator clicks a Zahlungsempfänger row to select it — it is highlighted in green.
8. Operator clicks 'Nach ALLEGRO übernehmen' button.
9. sendMessage() deep-clones the selected person, replaces the zahlungsempfaenger array with only the selected entry.
10. The JSON message { target: 'textfield', content: person+payment } is sent via WebSocket to Node.js relay.
11. WebsocketServer.js broadcasts the message to all connected clients including the Allegro Swing application.
12. Allegro Swing client (if connected) receives the person and payment data.

workflows.description: Any text typed in the Vue.js textarea is automatically broadcast via WebSocket to all connected clients.
steps: 1. Operator types in the textarea in the Vue.js Search component.
2. Vue reactive watcher on internal_content_textarea fires on any change.
3. sendMessage(val, 'textarea') is called with the new textarea content.
4. JSON message { target: 'textarea', content: text } is sent to Node.js WebSocket relay.
5. All connected clients receive the textarea content update.

field_catalogue.editable: true in Swing; false/read-only in Vue
true in Swing; false/read-only in Vue
true in Swing; read-only in Vue (from Zahlungsempfänger data)

field_catalogue.note: Populated with HTTP response body after form submission in Swing; broadcast via WebSocket on change in Vue

key_insights: The application is a modernisation PoC demonstrating how a legacy Java Swing desktop application (Allegro) can be complemented by a modern Vue.js web frontend via real-time WebSocket communication.

Gender is represented as three independent Boolean flags rather than a single enum, which simplifies API serialisation (all fields become strings) but requires UI-layer mutual exclusivity enforcement via ButtonGroup.
The Vue.js search component intentionally disables IBAN/BIC manual entry — banking data can only be populated by selecting from the structured Zahlungsempfänger list, enforcing data integrity.
The person search uses OR logic across all criteria, meaning a single matching criterion is sufficient to include a record — this is optimised for recall over precision.
The Node.js relay uses a broadcast-all model with no message filtering, which means all connected clients (both Swing and Vue instances) receive all messages — appropriate for a two-party PoC.

The HTTP submission endpoint uses httpbin (an echo service) as the backend mock, meaning the response always mirrors the request — form validation and server-side business rules are outside the PoC scope.
Real-time bidirectional binding in the MVP presenter ensures zero-lag model synchronisation — no explicit 'apply' or 'save' step exists before the final submission action.
